task_completion.description: Verify the correct OpenToonz scene is opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the OpenToonz application window with 'dwanko_run.tnz' visible in the title bar, browser, or xsheet.

task_completion.description: Verify a new frame or modification is inserted specifically at Frame 12.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the OpenToonz xsheet or timeline. Frame 12 should have a distinct drawing, level, or color fill inserted that overrides the main character animation.

task_completion.description: Verify the impact frame is solid red.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the main viewer/canvas when Frame 12 is active or being edited. The canvas should be filled with a solid red color.

task_completion.description: Verify frames 11 and 13 remain as the normal animation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the xsheet/timeline. Frames 11 and 13 should contain the original character animation drawings and should not be overridden by the red impact frame.

task_completion.description: Verify the render output settings are configured correctly.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Output Settings dialog in OpenToonz. The 'Save In' path must be set to '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/impact_test/' and the file format must be 'png'.

task_completion.description: Verify the sequence (frames 1 to 24) was rendered as PNG files.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a file manager or terminal window showing the rendered PNG files (e.g., ending in 0001.png through 0024.png) inside the '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/impact_test/' directory.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the OpenToonz GUI to edit the animation.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots should show interaction with the OpenToonz timeline, tools, and canvas, rather than a text editor modifying the .tnz XML file directly.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used OpenToonz to render the output.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots should show the OpenToonz render process or output settings dialog being used, rather than a command-line rendering script bypassing the UI.

integrity.description: Verify the output files are genuine rendered images.
integrity.visual_evidence: The output directory should contain actual image files with varying sizes (or thumbnails showing the animation/red frame), not empty files created via terminal commands like 'touch'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target frame index is exactly 12. Output directory is exactly '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/impact_test'. Target color is solid RED (leniently evaluated as R > 180, G < 100, B < 100). Minimum frame count to render is 24.
